acceptance: grep -c 'same transaction' ADR-003 returns 0 for lines describing V1 audit write behavior (references to durable backend same-transaction goal are acceptable); diagram shows begin_tx() before version check; risk mitigation references post-commit strategy and recovery invariant
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-003-backing-store-architecture.md:67,169,252,267
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f3262b1 updated the audit write path section of ADR-003 to describe the post-commit audit strategy, but four other sections in the same document still claim audit writes happen in the same storage transaction as entity writes:
  (1) Line 67: Atomicity bullet still says audit is in the same transaction
  (2) Line 169: StorageAdapter key design points says entity and audit writes share a transaction handle
  (3) Line 252: Consequences says audit overhead is from append in same transaction
  (4) Line 267: Risk mitigation says audit and entity writes share a transaction
Additionally, the audit write path diagram (lines 72-89) shows StorageAdapter.begin_tx() after version check and entity writes, but code calls begin_tx() first (transaction.rs:149).
Suggested fix: Update all four locations to reference the post-commit strategy. Fix the diagram to show begin_tx() before version check. Update the risk mitigation to reference the recovery invariant instead of shared-transaction atomicity.
title: correctness: ADR-003 has residual same-transaction audit claims after post-commit update

acceptance: handle_put_schema creates an audit entry with MutationType::SchemaUpdate; PutSchemaRequest includes actor field; test exists asserting audit entry is written after put_schema
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:519-525
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: handle_put_schema stores a schema without creating an audit entry. MutationType::SchemaUpdate exists in the audit system but is never used. Additionally PutSchemaRequest lacks an actor field, so audit provenance cannot be recorded. Violates Principle P2 (100% mutation coverage: no code path mutates state without audit entry).
Suggested fix: Add actor: Option<String> to PutSchemaRequest, emit an AuditEntry with MutationType::SchemaUpdate in handle_put_schema before returning.
title: correctness: handle_put_schema missing audit entry

acceptance: PUT /collections/{name}/schema accepts an optional actor field in the request body; gateway integration test asserts audit entry actor matches the provided value; existing put_schema tests continue to pass
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:505-522
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The put_schema gateway endpoint accepts Json<CollectionSchema> directly (line 508) and hardcodes actor: None when constructing PutSchemaRequest (line 518). Every other mutating endpoint uses a custom body struct with actor: Option<String> (CreateEntityBody, UpdateEntityBody, DeleteEntityBody, etc.) and passes body.actor through. This means REST API callers cannot provide audit provenance for schema updates — the audit actor will always be 'anonymous'. The handler layer supports actor (added in hx-2a25bfc6) but the HTTP layer does not pass it through.
Suggested fix: Create a PutSchemaBody wrapper struct (containing schema fields plus actor: Option<String>), use it as the Json extractor type in put_schema, and pass body.actor into PutSchemaRequest. Add a gateway integration test verifying actor is recorded in the audit entry after PUT /collections/{name}/schema.
title: integration: gateway put_schema endpoint does not pass actor to handler

acceptance: AxonHandler constructed with a pre-populated StorageAdapter (containing entities and schemas) correctly reports those collections via list_collections and describe_collection; test: create collection, drop and reconstruct AxonHandler, verify list_collections returns the collection
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:38
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: collections: HashSet<CollectionId> is an in-memory data structure not persisted via StorageAdapter. When using a durable backend (SQLite), entities and schemas survive restart but the collection registry does not. After restart, list_collections returns empty, describe_collection returns NotFound, and drop_collection returns NotFound — even though entities and schemas still exist in storage. FEAT-001 NFR states: Durability: Collection metadata is durable — survives process restart in both embedded and server modes.
Suggested fix: Add collection metadata persistence to the StorageAdapter trait (e.g. list_collections, register_collection, unregister_collection) and load the set on AxonHandler construction, or reconstruct from stored schemas at startup.
title: correctness: collection registry is ephemeral — not persisted via StorageAdapter

acceptance: Either: (1) Transaction::create/update/delete reject the 101st op with AxonError::InvalidArgument AND Transaction struct carries created_at with timeout enforcement in commit path; OR (2) ADR-004 Implementation Notes clearly mark these as not-yet-implemented with a reference to the implementing issue
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:103-110,210-211
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs (Transaction struct and commit method)
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: ADR-004 lines 103-110 state that the 101st stage_* call returns InvalidArgument and that the Transaction struct carries a creation timestamp for 30s timeout enforcement. Lines 210-211 say 'The 100-op limit and 30s timeout are checked in the commit path per FEAT-008.' However, transaction.rs has no op count check in create/update/delete methods, no created_at timestamp field on the Transaction struct, and no timeout check in commit/execute. The Implementation Notes read as descriptions of current state, not future work.
Suggested fix: Either (a) implement the 100-op limit check in stage methods and add a created_at field with timeout check in commit, or (b) move these items from Implementation Notes to a 'Not Yet Implemented' section to avoid misleading readers.
title: correctness: ADR-004 Implementation Notes claim 100-op limit and 30s timeout exist but they are not implemented

acceptance: ADR-004 Protocol section Commit sequence describes audit entries being flushed after commit_tx(), consistent with the Audit Integration section and the code in transaction.rs
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:48-56
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The Protocol section (lines 48-56) describes the commit path as: Apply all entity writes → Write all audit entries → Commit storage transaction. This claims audit entries are written BEFORE commit_tx(), directly contradicting the Audit Integration section (lines 92-113) updated in commit f3262b1, which correctly describes the post-commit audit strategy. A reader of the Protocol section gets the wrong mental model of when audit writes happen.
Suggested fix: Update the Protocol Commit sequence to: Apply all entity writes → commit_tx() → Flush buffered audit entries. Add a note that audit entries are written post-commit (reference the Audit Integration section).
title: correctness: ADR-004 Protocol section still claims pre-commit audit writes

acceptance: 1. Transaction::delete calls check_op_limit and returns Err when at MAX_OPS. 2. A test creates 100 delete ops then asserts the 101st delete returns InvalidArgument. 3. cargo test -p axon-api passes.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:129
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Transaction::delete does not call check_op_limit() before pushing a WriteOp, unlike create (line 91) and update (line 113). The docstring at line 128 claims it returns Err(InvalidArgument) if the transaction already has MAX_OPS operations, but the implementation never enforces this. Delete operations bypass the MAX_OPS=100 safety guard, allowing transactions to exceed the limit.
Suggested fix: Add self.check_op_limit()?; as the first statement in the delete method body, before constructing the sentinel entity.
title: correctness: Transaction::delete missing check_op_limit guard

acceptance: put_schema() with an invalid entity_schema (e.g. {"type": "bogus"}) returns AxonError::SchemaValidation; existing tests continue to pass
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:52-53,69-72
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The public put_schema() method (line 52) and the deprecated register_schema() method (line 69) on AxonHandler write schemas directly to storage without calling compile_entity_schema(). While handle_put_schema (the HTTP-facing handler) now validates, these helper methods allow callers to persist an invalid entity_schema that would cause confusing validation errors on subsequent entity writes. This is inconsistent with Principle P5 (Schema Earns Its Keep) which requires validation on every write path.
Suggested fix: Add compile_entity_schema() validation to put_schema(). For register_schema(), either add the same validation or remove the method if it is truly deprecated.
title: correctness: put_schema and register_schema bypass entity_schema validation

acceptance: A single version-monotonicity check function exists in axon-sim, imported by both audit_completeness and transaction_atomicity workloads; no duplicate implementations remain
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-sim/src/audit_completeness.rs:274 and crates/axon-sim/src/transaction_atomicity.rs:261
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: check_version_monotonicity (audit_completeness.rs) and check_entity_version_monotonicity (transaction_atomicity.rs) are identical logic with different names. This DRY violation means a bug fix or enhancement to one may not be applied to the other, risking divergence in INV-007 checking across workloads.
Suggested fix: Extract the shared version monotonicity checker into a common module (e.g., axon-sim/src/invariants.rs or a shared helper in lib.rs) and have both workloads import it.
title: correctness: deduplicate version monotonicity check in axon-sim workloads

acceptance: 1. FEAT-005-api-surface.md line 31 uses 'entity' not 'document' when referring to Axon data operations. 2. FEAT-005-api-surface.md line 41 heading uses 'Entity operations' not 'Document operations'. 3. CLI subcommand naming decision is documented.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7d905a7.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-005-api-surface.md:31,41
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 7d905a7 updated cross-reference lines in FEAT-005 but did not update body text. Line 31 still says 'collection, document, schema, and audit operations' (should be 'entity'). Line 41 still says 'Document operations: axon doc create|get|update|delete|list|query' — both the heading and the CLI subcommand name use 'document/doc' instead of 'entity'. This creates terminology inconsistency within the spec stack and may leak into CLI implementation.
Suggested fix: Change line 31 to 'collection, entity, schema, and audit operations'. Change line 41 heading to 'Entity operations'. Decide whether the CLI subcommand should be 'axon entity' or keep 'axon doc' as a shorthand (and document the decision).
title: correctness: FEAT-005 still uses 'document' terminology in body text

acceptance: gRPC version_conflict status detail includes current_entity JSON; cargo test includes a gRPC test asserting current_entity is present in conflict responses
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:38-41
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Spec: FEAT-004, FEAT-008
Description: The gRPC axon_to_status function silently drops the current_entity field from ConflictingVersion errors via the .. pattern. FEAT-004 (US-010 AC4) and FEAT-008 (US-021 AC2) both require that conflict responses include the current entity state so callers can merge and retry. The HTTP gateway correctly serializes current_entity in the 409 JSON body, but the gRPC response only includes expected/actual version numbers. This violates principle P6 (API parity between embedded and server modes).
Suggested fix: Add a current_entity field to the protobuf ConflictingVersion / error response message, serialize the entity into it in axon_to_status, and add a gRPC integration test verifying the field is present.
title: correctness: gRPC conflict response missing current_entity

acceptance: Test exists: begin_tx, put_schema, abort_tx leaves schemas unchanged; abort_tx restores schemas to pre-transaction state
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs:126-147
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: begin_tx() snapshots self.data but not self.schemas. If a transaction calls put_schema then abort_tx(), entity changes are rolled back but the schema change persists. This violates Principle P4 (Atomicity: all operations commit or none do).
Suggested fix: Include self.schemas in the transaction snapshot alongside self.data. Either create a combined snapshot struct or snapshot both HashMaps.
title: correctness: schema changes bypass MemoryStorageAdapter transaction rollback

acceptance: Test exists: put_schema with invalid JSON Schema (e.g. type bogus) returns AxonError::SchemaValidation; valid schemas still accepted
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:519-525
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: handle_put_schema stores arbitrary JSON as entity_schema without verifying it compiles as valid JSON Schema. A malformed schema silently persists and causes confusing validation errors on subsequent entity writes. Per Principle P5, schemas should earn their keep and the schema itself should be validated on write.
Suggested fix: Attempt to compile the entity_schema value with the jsonschema crate in handle_put_schema; reject with AxonError::SchemaValidation if it fails to compile.
title: correctness: handle_put_schema does not validate entity_schema as valid JSON Schema

acceptance: DELETE /links route exists in gateway.rs and correctly calls handler.delete_link; DeleteLink RPC exists in service.rs and axon.proto; an integration test creates then deletes a link via HTTP and verifies 200 response; cargo test -p axon-server passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs (missing route), crates/axon-server/src/service.rs (missing RPC), crates/axon-server/proto/axon.proto (missing messages)
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: create_link is exposed via POST /links (REST) and CreateLink (gRPC), but the new delete_link handler has no corresponding server-side endpoint. Network clients cannot invoke link deletion. The proto file also lacks DeleteLink RPC definition and DeleteLinkRequest/DeleteLinkResponse messages.
Suggested fix: (1) Add DELETE /links REST route in gateway.rs following the create_link wiring pattern. (2) Add DeleteLink RPC in service.rs and axon.proto with DeleteLinkRequest/DeleteLinkResponse proto messages. (3) Add integration test in gateway.rs verifying the round-trip.
title: integration: delete_link missing HTTP/gRPC server endpoints

acceptance: delete_entity does not call range_scan with unbounded parameters on the links collection; inbound link check uses a targeted query or index
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:158
Category: performance
Severity: medium
Description: delete_entity performs range_scan(&links_col, None, None, None) which loads ALL link entities into memory to check for inbound references. This is O(N) in total link count per delete call. As link volume grows, every entity delete becomes increasingly expensive.
Suggested fix: Add an indexed query or filtered scan to the StorageAdapter trait that supports querying links by target (collection, id), avoiding the full table scan. Alternatively, maintain a reverse-index of inbound links.
title: performance: delete_entity full table scan for inbound link check

acceptance: OneToOne rejects second link from same source with same type. ManyToOne rejects second link to same target with same type. Tests verify all cardinality constraints.
description: FEAT-007 and ADR-002 define Cardinality enum (OneToOne, OneToMany, ManyToOne, ManyToMany). Enum defined in axon-schema but not enforced during create_link.
title: Enforce link-type cardinality constraints at write time

acceptance: handle_put_schema calls self.put_schema() for persistence; compile_entity_schema appears in exactly one method (put_schema); all existing tests pass
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:526-528
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: handle_put_schema (line 526-528) and put_schema (line 54-56) both independently call compile_entity_schema(). handle_put_schema bypasses put_schema() entirely, calling self.storage.put_schema() directly at line 530. Two code paths must be kept in sync for schema validation logic. If validation is extended, one path could be updated without the other, creating a silent validation bypass.
Suggested fix: Refactor handle_put_schema to delegate schema persistence to self.put_schema() instead of self.storage.put_schema() directly. This requires cloning the schema before the call so it remains available for the audit entry and response. The validation in handle_put_schema (lines 526-528) can then be removed.
title: correctness: handle_put_schema duplicates validation logic from put_schema

acceptance: MutationType has LinkCreate and LinkDelete variants; create_link produces a link.create audit entry; delete_link produces a link.delete audit entry; tests verify audit entries via query_audit after each link operation; cargo test -p axon-api -p axon-audit passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:448-492
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The new delete_link handler mutates storage (deletes from __axon_links__ and __axon_links_rev__) but never calls self.audit.append(). This violates principle P2 (100% mutation coverage) and P3 (entity and link mutations produce identical audit trail quality). The actor field on DeleteLinkRequest is accepted but silently discarded. Additionally, MutationType lacks LinkCreate/LinkDelete variants. Pre-existing: create_link (line ~400-441) also has no audit entry — the new code followed a bad pattern.
Suggested fix: (1) Add LinkCreate and LinkDelete variants to MutationType in axon-audit/src/entry.rs with Display strings link.create and link.delete. (2) Add self.audit.append() in delete_link recording the link details as before state. (3) Add self.audit.append() in create_link recording the link as after state. (4) Add tests asserting audit entries exist after link create and delete operations.
title: correctness: delete_link (and create_link) missing audit trail entries

acceptance: Entity struct includes created_at, updated_at, created_by, updated_by. create_entity populates created_at/created_by. update_entity populates updated_at/updated_by. All existing tests still pass.
description: FEAT-004 specifies entities include _created_at, _updated_at, _created_by, _updated_by. Entity struct currently has only collection, id, version, data. Add system metadata fields and populate them in create/update handlers.
title: Add system metadata fields to Entity struct

acceptance: 1. prd.md line 314 says 'bead entity' not 'bead document'. 2. prd.md line 316 says 'matching entities' not 'matching documents'. 3. prd.md line 400 says 'querying entities' not 'querying documents'.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7d905a7.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md:314,316,400
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The PRD Primary Flow section (lines 314-316) uses 'document' as an Axon data model term: 'bead document' (line 314) and 'matching documents' (line 316). Line 400 says 'storing and querying documents'. Per the terminology decision (axon-1c6adf26), the Axon data model term is 'entity', not 'document'. The PRD is the highest-authority spec, so stale terminology here is more impactful than in feature specs. Note: other PRD uses of 'document' (document stores, document management, PRD is a living document) correctly refer to the English word and should NOT be changed.
Suggested fix: Line 314: 'bead document' -> 'bead entity'. Line 316: 'matching documents' -> 'matching entities'. Line 400: 'querying documents' -> 'querying entities'.
title: correctness: PRD still uses 'document' as Axon data model term in Primary Flow

acceptance: TraverseResponse includes path (sequence of entity/link pairs). Per-hop filter predicates supported. Reachability mode returns boolean and short-circuits on first path. Tests verify all three features.
description: FEAT-009 P1 gaps: (1) path reporting — return path sequence from source to each entity, (2) per-hop entity filtering — different filter predicates at each depth, (3) reachability query — boolean short-circuit. Current traverse returns entities only with link_type filter.
title: Implement FEAT-009 P1 traversal features: path reporting, per-hop filtering, reachability

acceptance: Design decision documented: either audit entries are only written after commit_tx succeeds, or the audit log documents that entries may correspond to rolled-back transactions.
description:
Learning from fresh-eyes review of commit 89314f0.

AuditLog::append is immediate and non-transactional. When Transaction::commit calls execute() and then either commit_tx() fails or execute() itself fails partway through, the storage-level transaction is rolled back via abort_tx(), but audit entries written during execute() persist.

This means the audit log can contain entries for mutations that were never committed. These entries have transaction_id set, but there is no status field to distinguish committed from rolled-back entries.

Evidence:
- AuditLog trait (crates/axon-audit/src/log.rs): append() is immediate, no rollback capability.
- Transaction::commit (crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:132-157): execute() writes audit entries before commit_tx is called.
- MemoryStorageAdapter::abort_tx restores snapshot, but audit log is separate.

Options to consider:
1. Buffer audit entries in Transaction and only append after commit_tx succeeds.
2. Add a status/outcome field to audit entries (committed vs rolled-back).
3. Accept as intentional and document the behavior.
title: design: audit log entries persist after transaction rollback

acceptance: Decision recorded. If defer: ADR-003 amended with rationale. If implement: execution issue created with scope and AC.
description: ADR-003 specifies SQLite + PostgreSQL for V1. PostgreSQL is required for server mode. Current scope is primarily embedded. Implementing PostgreSQL is significant work (sqlx integration, migration, connection pooling). Decision: implement now or update ADR-003 to defer.
title: Decision: PostgreSQL backend — V1 P0 or defer to P1?

acceptance: register_schema() removed or made non-public. All callers migrated to put_schema(). No panics in library code.
description: register_schema() is deprecated in favor of put_schema() but still public and panics on error, violating rust-cargo no-unwrap rule. Remove or make private.
title: Remove deprecated register_schema() method

acceptance: Transaction with >100 ops returns InvalidArgument error. Transaction exceeding 30s timeout aborts. Tests verify both limits.
description: FEAT-008 specifies max 100 ops per transaction and 30s timeout. Neither is enforced in transaction.rs.
title: Enforce transaction operation limit (100) and timeout (30s)

acceptance: http_update_version_conflict_returns_409 asserts body["detail"]["current_entity"] is non-null with correct id, version, and data fields; cargo test -p axon-server passes
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:715-732
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Spec: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs
Description: The HTTP gateway tests http_update_version_conflict_returns_409 and http_errors_are_structured_with_field_level_details do not assert that current_entity is present in 409 conflict responses. The gRPC path (service.rs) has two tests verifying current_entity for both Some and None cases, but the HTTP path lacks equivalent coverage. This creates a P6 (API parity) test-coverage gap between transports — a regression in the gateway serialization of current_entity would go undetected.
Suggested fix: Add assertions to http_update_version_conflict_returns_409 verifying body["detail"]["current_entity"] is non-null and contains the expected entity id, version, and data. Optionally add a second test for the null case.
title: integration: HTTP conflict tests missing current_entity assertions

acceptance: ADR-004 Protocol section commit sequence shows audit entries flushed after commit_tx(), not before. grep for 'Write all audit entries' in the Protocol block returns 0 or the line clearly indicates post-commit timing.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 5b749c1.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:53-55
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: ADR-004 Protocol section (lines 53-55) shows the commit sequence as: (1) Apply all entity writes, (2) Write all audit entries, (3) Commit storage transaction. This implies audit entries are written before commit_tx(), inside the storage transaction. However, ADR-004's own Audit Integration section (lines 93-94) correctly states audit entries are buffered and flushed only after commit_tx() succeeds. The code (transaction.rs:152-160) confirms post-commit flush. This is the same class of residual same-transaction audit claim that commit 5b749c1 fixed in ADR-003.
Suggested fix: Update the Protocol commit sequence to show audit entry buffering during execute, storage commit, then post-commit audit flush. E.g.: Apply all entity writes (version + 1) -> Commit storage transaction (atomic via StorageAdapter) -> Flush buffered audit entries (post-commit).
title: correctness: ADR-004 Protocol section describes pre-commit audit writes, contradicting post-commit strategy

acceptance: PROP-004 exercises at least two transactions with overlapping entity sets and verifies serializable final state
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: docs/helix/03-test/test-plan.md (PROP-004)
File: crates/axon-api/src/proptest_api.rs:108-175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The test plan specifies PROP-004 as verifying concurrent multi-entity transaction serializability. The implemented test only tests a single transaction with correct or incorrect versions. It does not test multiple overlapping transactions or verify serializable outcomes. Constrained by MemoryStorageAdapter requiring &mut self.
Suggested fix: When a concurrent-capable backend is available, add a test exercising overlapping transactions. In the interim, consider sequential simulation with random ordering.
title: correctness: PROP-004 tests single-transaction atomicity, not concurrent serializability

acceptance: Test: a FilterNode nested deeper than the configured max depth (e.g. 32) returns an AxonError::InvalidArgument. Test: a filter at exactly the max depth succeeds. No stack overflow on deeply nested input.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:664-668 and crates/axon-api/src/request.rs:149-154
Category: security
Severity: medium
Description: FilterNode is a recursive enum (And/Or variants contain Vec of FilterNode). The apply_filter function recurses without a depth limit. A malicious or buggy client can send a deeply nested filter via the HTTP API (thousands of levels of nesting), causing a stack overflow and crashing the server process. The HTTP gateway directly deserializes client-provided JSON into FilterNode with no depth validation.
Suggested fix: Add a depth limit (e.g. max 32 levels) validated at the start of query_entities before filter evaluation.
title: security: no recursion depth limit on FilterNode tree

acceptance: Either: (a) CreateCollectionRequest includes a schema field and create_collection persists it, with a test asserting create without schema returns an error; OR (b) FEAT-001 constraints section is updated to document the create-then-bind pattern
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:499-522, crates/axon-api/src/request.rs:218-221
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: FEAT-001 overview says a collection is a named schema-bound container. US-001 acceptance criteria requires schema validation before creation. FEAT-001 constraints state schema binding is required. However CreateCollectionRequest only has name and actor — no schema field. Collections are created without any schema binding. The bead description says explicit create with schema binding but schema is not part of the create flow.
Suggested fix: Either add schema CollectionSchema to CreateCollectionRequest and validate and persist it during creation, or document the intentional deviation and update FEAT-001 constraints.
title: correctness: create_collection does not bind a schema per FEAT-001

acceptance: Decision recorded. If defer: FEAT-004 updated. If implement: execution issue created.
description: FEAT-004 US-012 specifies PATCH operation as P0. Full replacement works but PATCH adds API complexity. Decision needed on whether to implement now or defer and update FEAT-004.
title: Decision: partial update (PATCH) — V1 P0 or defer?

acceptance: cargo run -p axon-server starts both servers. Configurable ports via flags or env. Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM. Health check endpoint responds.
description: FEAT-005 specifies server mode. axon-server has lib.rs, service.rs, gateway.rs but no main.rs. Needs binary that starts gRPC + HTTP with configurable ports and graceful shutdown.
title: Add axon-server binary entry point (main.rs)

acceptance: filter_depth uses an iterative (non-recursive) algorithm. Test: a FilterNode tree of depth 100_000 returns the correct depth value without stack overflow.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:686-692
Spec: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs
Category: security/correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The filter_depth() function uses recursion to measure FilterNode tree depth. If an attacker constructs a tree deep enough to overflow the stack (e.g. millions of levels via a non-JSON path), the overflow occurs inside filter_depth before the depth check at line 228 can reject it. The commit message for 9d63b5a claims 'safe linear traversal' but the implementation recurses. Practical risk is mitigated by serde_json's default recursion limit (128) for HTTP deserialization, and 1000 levels works in tests. But defense-in-depth is incomplete — the guard itself shares the vulnerability it protects against.
Suggested fix: Rewrite filter_depth as an iterative traversal using an explicit Vec<&FilterNode> stack, eliminating recursive calls.
title: security: filter_depth itself uses unbounded recursion

acceptance: ADR-004 references the correct error variant names matching crates/axon-core/src/error.rs: ConflictingVersion (not Conflict) and SchemaValidation (not SchemaViolation)
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:69,73
File: crates/axon-core/src/error.rs:20,13
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: ADR-004 line 69 says 'Axon returns AxonError::Conflict' but the actual variant is AxonError::ConflictingVersion (error.rs:20). Line 73 says 'AxonError::SchemaViolation with retryable: false' but the actual variant is AxonError::SchemaValidation (error.rs:13) and neither variant has a retryable field. These naming mismatches will confuse implementers who try to match the ADR specification against the code.
Suggested fix: Update ADR-004 to use the correct variant names (ConflictingVersion, SchemaValidation). If the retryable field is intended as a future addition, note it as such or file a separate implementation issue.
title: correctness: ADR-004 references AxonError::Conflict and SchemaViolation but actual variants are ConflictingVersion and SchemaValidation

acceptance: 1. Transaction::commit calls abort_tx() when commit_tx() fails. 2. A test exists that simulates commit_tx failure and verifies abort_tx is called and the adapter is not wedged.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:141-143
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Spec: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs
Description: In Transaction::commit, when execute() succeeds but commit_tx() fails, no abort_tx() is called. The storage-level transaction is left open with uncommitted writes buffered. For SqliteStorageAdapter, in_tx stays true, wedging the adapter so subsequent begin_tx() calls are rejected. This violates P4 (Transactions Mean Transactions) — a failed commit should leave the system in a clean state.
Suggested fix: Wrap commit_tx() failure to call abort_tx() before propagating the error:
  Ok(written) => match storage.commit_tx() {
      Ok(()) => Ok(written),
      Err(e) => { let _ = storage.abort_tx(); Err(e) }
  }
title: correctness: abort_tx not called on commit_tx failure in Transaction::commit

acceptance: Test: query_entities with an after_id that does not exist in the collection returns an error (not silently reset results). Existing pagination tests continue to pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:271-278
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: When after_id references an entity not in the filtered result set (deleted between pages, filter changed, or invalid), position().map(|i| i + 1).unwrap_or(0) silently falls back to index 0, restarting pagination from the beginning. Clients cannot distinguish page 1 from an invalid-cursor restart. This causes silent duplicate processing during paginated iteration.
Suggested fix: Return AxonError::InvalidArgument when after_id is provided but not found in the result set, or add a cursor_valid field to the response.
title: correctness: invalid cursor silently resets pagination to start

acceptance: delete_link handler (when implemented) removes both __axon_links__ and __axon_links_rev__ entries; inject_isolation_violation deletes both forward and reverse-index entries; no orphaned reverse-index entries remain after link deletion in any test
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:437, crates/axon-sim/src/cycle.rs:270
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: create_link writes reverse-index entries to __axon_links_rev__ but no code path removes them when links are deleted. The inject_isolation_violation test helper deletes forward links from __axon_links__ without cleaning up the reverse-index, leaving orphaned entries that would cause delete_entity to falsely reject deletions. When a delete_link handler is implemented, reverse-index cleanup must be included.
Suggested fix: (1) When building the delete_link handler, delete the reverse-index entry alongside the forward link using Link::rev_storage_id(). (2) Update inject_isolation_violation in cycle.rs to also delete the __axon_links_rev__ entry to keep test state consistent.
title: correctness: reverse-index entries orphaned on link deletion

acceptance: No route path conflicts: creating and retrieving an entity with ID query works correctly. Query endpoint is accessible at a non-conflicting path. HTTP integration tests cover both cases.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs:507
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: /entities/{collection}/query (POST) is registered alongside /entities/{collection}/{id} (GET/PUT/DELETE/POST). Axum matchit router prioritizes literal segments over captures. Any request to /entities/<col>/query regardless of method matches the literal route first. GET /entities/tasks/query (fetching entity with ID query) returns 405 instead of the entity. POST /entities/tasks/query (creating entity with ID query) routes to the query handler instead. This breaks API consistency for the reserved ID query and conflicts with POST /entities/{collection}/{id} for entity creation.
Suggested fix: Move query endpoint to a non-conflicting path such as POST /collections/{collection}/query or POST /query/{collection}.
title: integration: route path conflict — query literal shadows entity {id} capture

acceptance: Either: (1) audit entries are written within the StorageAdapter transaction before commit_tx(), verified by a test that crashes between commit and audit-append and shows no gap; OR (2) ADR-003 and ADR-004 explicitly document the post-commit audit strategy with a documented recovery mechanism, and INV-003 audit completeness invariant accounts for the gap
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-004-transaction-model.md:91-92
File: crates/axon-api/src/transaction.rs:144-151
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: ADR-004 (line 91-92) and ADR-003 (audit write path diagram) both specify that audit entries are written in the same storage transaction as entity mutations, ensuring atomic commit/rollback. However, transaction.rs writes audit entries AFTER commit_tx() succeeds (line 144-151). The code comment explicitly says 'The storage transaction is already durable at this point, so audit failures do not undo the committed data.' This creates a crash-safety gap: if the process dies between commit_tx() and audit append, committed mutations have no audit trail. This contradicts principle P2 (Audit is Not Optional - 100% mutation coverage).
Suggested fix: Either (a) refactor the AuditLog to write through the StorageAdapter transaction handle so audit entries are part of the same storage transaction, or (b) update ADR-003 and ADR-004 to document the post-commit audit strategy and add a recovery mechanism (e.g., write-ahead intent log or audit reconciliation on startup).
title: correctness: audit entries written post-commit contradicts ADR-003/ADR-004 same-transaction guarantee

acceptance: A test exists that calls create_collection with an invalid entity_schema, asserts the call fails, and then asserts the collection does NOT appear in list_collections (i.e. no orphan registration).
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: crates/axon-api/src/handler.rs:525-526
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: create_collection calls register_collection (line 525) then put_schema (line 526) sequentially. If put_schema fails (e.g. invalid entity_schema that fails compile_entity_schema), the collection is left registered but schemaless. This violates the documented invariant 'schemaless collections are not supported' (FEAT-001). A subsequent create_collection call for the same name would fail with AlreadyExists. The user would need to drop_collection and retry. The same atomicity gap exists with the audit append on line 528 — a schema+collection could be persisted with no audit trail.
Suggested fix: Either (a) validate the schema before calling register_collection (move put_schema or at least compile_entity_schema before the register call), or (b) roll back register_collection on put_schema failure. Option (a) is simpler and sufficient since validation is the likely failure mode.
notes: BLOCKED [2026-04-05T18:03:27-04:00]: intractable after 4 attempts with exponential backoff
title: correctness: create_collection is not atomic — put_schema failure leaves orphan collection

acceptance: All four stale references listed above are updated to use 'entity'. No legitimate 'document' references (document stores, document management domain) are changed. grep -w 'document' across FEAT-006, PRD, and principles.md returns only domain-concept or external-system references.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Remaining stale 'document' references after commit 9f65ea7 fixed PRD lines 314, 316, 400:
  - FEAT-006 line 20: 'collection/document primitives' → 'collection/entity primitives'
  - FEAT-006 line 30: 'bead documents' → 'bead entities'
  - PRD line 415: 'single-doc operations' → 'single-entity operations'
  - principles.md line 75: 'Invalid documents' → 'Invalid entities'
Note: references to 'document stores' (describing other systems), 'document management' (domain concept), and comparison table entries are intentional and should NOT be changed.
title: integration: incomplete document→entity terminology migration across spec stack

acceptance: Running 'cargo check -p axon-storage' at every commit in the chain succeeds without errors
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: crates/axon-storage/src/lib.rs
File: crates/axon-storage/src/lib.rs:8
Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: Commit 05874a8 adds 'pub mod conformance;' to crates/axon-storage/src/lib.rs but does not include conformance.rs in the commit. A clean checkout of this commit fails to compile. The file is only added in the subsequent commit 90d9f18. This breaks the build chain for CI, bisection, or checkout at this commit.
Suggested fix: Squash commits 05874a8 and 90d9f18, or rebase to move the module declaration into the commit that adds the file.
title: correctness: commit 05874a8 breaks build — pub mod conformance without conformance.rs

acceptance: PROP-002 test generates sequences including delete ops and verifies audit log replay produces correct final state
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
Spec: docs/helix/03-test/test-plan.md (PROP-002)
File: crates/axon-api/src/proptest_api.rs:31-96
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The test plan specifies PROP-002 as covering any sequence of CRUD operations. The implemented test only generates create+update sequences. Delete operations are never exercised, so audit reconstruction for entity deletion is unverified. The test also checks only the last audit entry rather than replaying the full log.
Suggested fix: Extend the proptest strategy to include delete operations and add a replay loop that walks the full audit log to reconstruct final state.
title: correctness: PROP-002 does not test audit reconstruction for delete operations

acceptance: Test suite in axon-server exercises all gRPC and HTTP endpoints. Validates response structure. Validates error format. Tests pass for all operations.
description: Test plan L6: gRPC methods match protobuf, HTTP gateway identical to gRPC, structured errors, embedded API parity. axon-server has 0 tests.
title: Implement L6 API contract tests for gRPC and HTTP gateway

acceptance: [workspace.lints] configured in root Cargo.toml. All crates opt in via [lints] workspace = true. cargo-deny configured with deny.toml. Both pass in CI.
description: rust-cargo concern requires [workspace.lints] with clippy pedantic/nursery and cargo-deny. Both tracked as known overrides in concerns.md but should be resolved.
title: Configure workspace lints and cargo-deny per rust-cargo concern
